---
title: "Compute Embedded Costs"
---

Implements sections 2.3.3, 2.3.4, and 2.4 of
Measuring Cross-Subsidization for Seasonal Heat Pump Rates: Cost-of-Service Methodology Analysis
https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/16nlFAL_RwGl_INAJrhR5irgnNC-gj9zF

### IMPORTS
```{python}

    from buildstock_fetch.scenarios import uniform_adoption
    from buildstock_fetch.mixed_upgrade import MixedUpgradeScenario
    from buildstock_fetch.read import BuildStockRead
    from cloudpathlib import S3Path
    import polars as pl

    from typing import Literal

    ScenarioType = Literal["existing", "mixed_upgrade"]
```

```{python}
    state = "RI"
    release = "res_2024_amy2018_2"
    data_path = S3Path("s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/")

    random_seed=67
    additional_hp_adoption_pct = 0.1 # 0.0 = current; specify only as integer, for single-year analysis
    upgrade_id = 2
    start_year = 2028

    # sample_n = 50 # for debugging only
```

## HELPER FUNCTIONS
## TODO: MOVE THIS INTO A HELPER FILE
```{python}
    def adjust_timestamp(lazy_df: pl.LazyFrame, start_year: int, handle_leap_years: bool = False) -> pl.LazyFrame:
        """
        Adjusts the timestamp column in a lazy Polars DataFrame so that each 'year' value corresponds
        to the specified start_year plus its offset.

        For example, if start_year=2018:
            - rows with year==0: timestamps correspond to 2018
            - rows with year==1: timestamps are shifted to 2019
            and so on.

        Args:
            lazy_df: Polars LazyFrame with 'timestamp' and 'year' columns.
            start_year: The baseline year (integer, e.g., 2018)
            handle_leap_years: Whether to handle leap years by inserting a duplicate of Feb 28 data as Feb 29

        Returns:
            A new LazyFrame with the 'timestamp' column adjusted so that each 'year' is mapped
            to start_year + year, preserving month/day/hour/minute.
        """

        # Adjust the year of each timestamp so that it matches start_year + year
        updated_df= lazy_df.rename(
            {"timestamp": "original_timestamp",
            "year": "year_idx"}
            ).with_columns(
                pl.col("year_idx").add(start_year).alias("year")
            ).with_columns(
            pl.col("original_timestamp").dt.replace(year=pl.col("year")).alias("timestamp")
        )

        if handle_leap_years:
            # NOTE: Handle leap years by inserting a duplicate of Feb 28 data as Feb 29
            # We maybe exclude this to make data more generally interpretable across years?
            # Insert a duplicate of Feb 28 data as Feb 29 in leap years
            feb_28 = (
                updated_df
                .filter((pl.col("timestamp").dt.month() == 2) &
                        (pl.col("timestamp").dt.day() == 28) &
                        ((pl.col("year") % 4) == 0))
            )

            # Create Feb 29 timestamps by changing day = 29 on a copy of Feb 28 rows
            feb_29 = (
                feb_28
                .with_columns(
                    pl.col("timestamp").dt.replace(day=29).alias("timestamp"),
                )
            )

            # Concatenate the original updated_df with the new Feb 29 rows
            updated_df = pl.concat([updated_df, feb_29]).sort("timestamp")

        return updated_df

    def _get_heating_type_distribution(sbmeta: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
        return sbmeta.collect().group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type").agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("total_homes")).with_columns((pl.col("total_homes") / pl.col("total_homes").sum()).alias("pct_of_total"))

    def _merge_weights(ldf: pl.LazyFrame, metadata: pl.DataFrame = pl.DataFrame()) -> pl.LazyFrame:
        return ldf.join(metadata.select(["bldg_id", "weight", "postprocess_group.heating_type"]), on="bldg_id", how="left")

    def _read_mus(scenario: dict[int, list[float]], scenario_name: str, baseline_metadata: pl.LazyFrame, data_path : S3Path, release: str, state: str | None, random_seed: int | None = None, sample_n: int | None = None, start_year: int | None = None):
        mus = MixedUpgradeScenario(
            scenario_name=scenario_name,
            data_path=data_path,
            release=release,
            states=state,
            # NOTE:
            # this is maybe weird, in that the set
            # of adopting households in BAU isn't a subset of the set
            # of adopting households in HP
            random=random_seed,
            sample_n=sample_n,
            scenario=scenario,
        )
        mus_metadata = mus.read_metadata()
        sbmeta = (
            baseline_metadata
            .join(
                mus_metadata.rename({"upgrade_id": "upgrade_mus"}),
                on="bldg_id",
                how="left"
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.col("upgrade_mus").fill_null(pl.col("upgrade")).alias("upgrade")
            )
            .drop("upgrade_mus")
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(pl.col("upgrade") > 0).then(pl.lit("heat_pump")).otherwise(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type")).alias("postprocess_group.heating_type")
            )
        )
        # NOTE: should this be piped for something something efficiency
        ldf = mus.read_load_curve_hourly().pipe(
            lambda ldf: adjust_timestamp(ldf, start_year=start_year)
        ).pipe(lambda ldf: _merge_weights(ldf, metadata=sbmeta))
        return mus, sbmeta, ldf
```

## LOAD METADATA
```{python}
    metadata_path = data_path / release / "metadata" / f"state={state}" / "upgrade=00" / "metadata-sb.parquet"
    if  metadata_path.exists():
        baseline_sbmeta = pl.scan_parquet(str(metadata_path))
    else:
        metadata_path = data_path / release / "metadata" / f"state={state}" / "upgrade=00" / "metadata.parquet"
        baseline_metadata = pl.scan_parquet(str(metadata_path))
        baseline_sbmeta = baseline_metadata.with_columns(
           pl.when(pl.any_horizontal(
            [pl.col("in.hvac_heating_type_and_fuel").str.contains(s, literal=True) for s in ("MSHP", "ASHP", "GSHP")]
        )).then(pl.lit("heat_pump")).otherwise(pl.lit("non-heat_pump")).alias("postprocess_group.heating_type")
        )

    # Compute percent heat_pump for postprocess_group.heating_type, using weights
    heating_type_distribution = _get_heating_type_distribution(baseline_sbmeta)
    current_hp_pct = heating_type_distribution.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump").select("pct_of_total").item()
    print(f"Current hp percent: {current_hp_pct:0.2f}")

    scenario = uniform_adoption(
        upgrade_ids=[upgrade_id],
        weights={upgrade_id: 1.0},
        # scale up the additional hp adoption percentage to account for the fact that
        # some of the homes randomly selected for upgrades will have already had heat pumps
        adoption_trajectory=[additional_hp_adoption_pct  / (1 - current_hp_pct)],
    )

    # Read 15-minute load curves as lazy dataframe
    # bau_mus, ldf_bau_loads = _get_load_curves(bau_scenario, "bau")
    mus, sbmeta, ldf_load_curves = _read_mus(scenario, "default", baseline_sbmeta, data_path, release, state, random_seed, sample_n, start_year)

    if additional_hp_adoption_pct > 0:
        heating_type_distribution = _get_heating_type_distribution(sbmeta)

        hp_pct = heating_type_distribution.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump").select("pct_of_total").item()
        print(f"Total hp percent: {hp_pct:0.2f}")
```

### COMPUTE LOAD FACTOR BY HP vs NON-HP
```{python}
    # Join load_curves with metadata to get heating_type for each home
    load_curves_with_meta = ldf_load_curves.join(sbmeta.select(["bldg_id", "postprocess_group.heating_type", "weight"]), on="bldg_id", how="left")

    # Aggregate load curves by heating type and timestamp
    aggregate_curves = (
        load_curves_with_meta.group_by(["timestamp", "postprocess_group.heating_type"])
        .agg([
            (pl.col("out.electricity.net.energy_consumption") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("electricity_kwh"),
            pl.col("weight").sum().alias("num_homes"),
        ])
    )

    # Now, for each heating_type, calculate the load factor:
    # For each group, get the sum of electricity_kwh over all timestamps, and also the max for that group
    load_factor_df = (
        aggregate_curves.group_by(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump")
        .agg([
            pl.col("num_homes").max().alias("num_homes"), # should be one unique value for each group
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("annual_electricity_kwh"),
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").max().alias("ncp_kw"),
        ])
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("annual_electricity_kwh") / (pl.col("ncp_kw") * 8760)).alias("load_factor")
        )
    ).collect()

    print(f"Data for state: {state}")
    print(load_factor_df)
```

## Compute NCPs
```{python}
    # Find the peak load hour (max electricity_kwh) for each heating_type,
    # also include the number of homes in each group
    peak_loads = (
        aggregate_curves
        .group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type")
        .agg([
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").max().alias("peak_load_kw"),
            pl.col("num_homes").max().alias("num_homes"),  # max since num_homes per group should be constant
        ])
        .collect()
    )

    # Sum of peak loads (NCP) for all heating types
    total_ncp = peak_loads["peak_load_kw"].sum()

    print(f"Total system NCP (sum of heating type peak loads): {total_ncp:.2f} kW")

    # Add percent of total NCP for each heating_type,
    # and calculate pct_of_total_ncp per home (divide by num_homes)
    peak_loads_pct = peak_loads.with_columns([
        (pl.col("peak_load_kw") / total_ncp * 100).alias("pct_of_total_ncp"),
        ((pl.col("peak_load_kw") / total_ncp * 100) / pl.col("num_homes")).alias("pct_of_total_ncp_per_home"),
    ])

    print(f"Data for state: {state}")
    print("Peak load (NCP) by heating type:")
    print(peak_loads_pct.select([
        "postprocess_group.heating_type",
        "peak_load_kw",
        "num_homes",
        "pct_of_total_ncp",
        "pct_of_total_ncp_per_home"
    ]))


```




## ############################################################################################################
## ############################################################################################################
## ############################################################################################################
## ############################################################################################################
## ############################################################################################################
## DEBUGGING

```{python}
    df_agg = aggregate_curves.collect()
    print(df_agg.head())
```

```{python}
    from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_line, labs, theme_minimal, scale_color_brewer

    df_per_home = df_agg.with_columns(
        (pl.col("electricity_kwh") / pl.col("num_homes")).alias("electricity_kwh"),
    )

    # Convert to pandas DataFrame for plotnine
    pdf = df_per_home.to_pandas()

    p = (
        ggplot(pdf, aes(x="timestamp", y="electricity_kwh", color="postprocess_group.heating_type"))
        + geom_line()
        + labs(
            x="Timestamp",
            y="Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
            color="Heating Type",
            title="Hourly Electricity Consumption by Heating Type"
        )
        + scale_color_brewer(type="qual", palette="Set1")
        + labs(
            title=f"Hourly Electricity Consumption by Heating Type - {state}"
        )
        + theme_minimal()
    )
    p.show()
```




### TRY AGAIN WITH A MIXED UPGRADE SCENARIO



```{python}
    random_seed=67
    upgrade_id = 2
    sample_n=200     # NOTE: DEBUGGING ONLY # downsample for debugging

    start_year = 2028

    bau_scenario = uniform_adoption(
        upgrade_ids=[upgrade_id],
        weights={upgrade_id: 1.0},
        adoption_trajectory=[0.0],
    )

    hp_scenario = uniform_adoption(
        upgrade_ids=[upgrade_id],
        weights={upgrade_id: 1.0},
        adoption_trajectory=[0.5],
    )

    def _merge_weights(ldf, metadata = pl.DataFrame()):
        return ldf.join(metadata.select(["bldg_id", "weight", "postprocess_group.heating_type"]), on="bldg_id", how="left")

    # NOTE: add typehint
    def _get_load_curves(scenario, scenario_name):
        mus = MixedUpgradeScenario(
            scenario_name=scenario_name,
            data_path=data_path,
            release=release,
            states=state,
            # NOTE:
            # this is maybe weird, in that the set
            # of adopting households in BAU isn't a subset of the set
            # of adopting households in HP
            random=random_seed,
            sample_n=sample_n,
            scenario=scenario,
        )
        # NOTE: should this be piped for something something efficiency
        ldf = mus.read_load_curve_hourly().pipe(
            lambda ldf: adjust_timestamp(ldf, start_year=start_year)
        ).pipe(lambda ldf: _merge_weights(ldf, metadata=sbmeta))
        return mus, ldf

    # Read 15-minute load curves as lazy dataframe
    # bau_mus, ldf_bau_loads = _get_load_curves(bau_scenario, "bau")
    hp_mus, ldf_hp_loads = _get_load_curves(hp_scenario, "hp")
```
```{python}
    ldf_hp_loads = ldf_hp_loads.with_columns(
        # Where upgrade > 0, overwrite postprocess_group.heating_type to "heat_pump"
        pl.when(pl.col("upgrade") > 0)
        .then(pl.lit("heat_pump"))
        .otherwise(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type"))
        .alias("postprocess_group.heating_type")
    )

    # Aggregate load curves by heating type and timestamp
    aggregate_curves = (
        ldf_hp_loads.group_by(["timestamp", "postprocess_group.heating_type"])
        .agg([
            (pl.col("out.electricity.total.energy_consumption") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("electricity_kwh"),
            pl.col("weight").sum().alias("num_homes"),
        ])
    )
```

```{python}
    # Now, for each heating_type, calculate the load factor:
    # For each group, get the sum of electricity_kwh over all timestamps, and also the max for that group
    load_factor_df = (
        aggregate_curves.group_by(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump")
        .agg([
            pl.col("num_homes").max().alias("num_homes"), # should be one unique value for each group
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("annual_electricity_kwh"),
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").max().alias("ncp_kw"),
        ])
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("annual_electricity_kwh") / (pl.col("ncp_kw") * 8760)).alias("load_factor")
        )
    ).collect()

    print(load_factor_df)
```

## DEBUGGING

```{python}
    df_hp_loads = ldf_hp_loads.collect()
    print(df_hp_loads.head())
```

```{python}
    print(ldf_hp_loads.select("upgrade").collect().get_column("upgrade").value_counts())
```

```{python}
    df_agg = aggregate_curves.collect()
    print(df_agg.head())
```

```{python}
    from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_line, labs, theme_minimal, scale_color_brewer

    df_per_home = df_agg.with_columns(
        (pl.col("electricity_kwh") / pl.col("num_homes")).alias("electricity_kwh"),
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(df_per_home, aes(x="timestamp", y="electricity_kwh", color="postprocess_group.heating_type"))
        + geom_line()
        + labs(
            x="Timestamp",
            y="Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
            color="Heating Type",
            title="Hourly Electricity Consumption by Heating Type"
        )
        + scale_color_brewer(type="qual", palette="Set1")
        + theme_minimal()
    )
    p.show()
```

```{python}
    from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_line, labs, theme_minimal, scale_color_brewer

    df_per_home = df_agg.with_columns(
        (pl.col("electricity_kwh") / pl.col("num_homes")).alias("electricity_kwh"),
        (pl.col("timestamp").dt.date()).alias("date"),
    )

    pdf_daily = (
        df_per_home.group_by(["date", "postprocess_group.heating_type"])
        .agg(pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("electricity_kwh"))
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(pdf_daily, aes(x="date", y="electricity_kwh", color="postprocess_group.heating_type"))
        + geom_line()
        + labs(
            x="Date",
            y="Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
            color="Heating Type",
            title="Daily Electricity Consumption by Heating Type"
        )
        + scale_color_brewer(type="qual", palette="Set1")
        + theme_minimal()
    )
    p.show()
```

```{python}
    df_per_home.group_by(["postprocess_group.heating_type"]).agg(pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("electricity_kwh"))
```
